sections.Name: Innova
sections.Psychological Profile: True neutral, Inno is very aware theres no such thing as right and wrong anymore in Night City, theres only biz. Make eddies, run the net, and keep his head attached to his shoulders at all costs. At least… before the cyberware drove him to the egde. The chrome. Its just a little more, he can take it. He’s gotta get better, he’s gotta be the best to make money and get bigger, more chrome, more money, more power, more security. Thats how it goes. You hear that…?
sections.Physical Description: He’s tall, standing at a solid 6’10” after so much body modding and enhancements. A dragon-like exotic human, he’s covered in tattoos of the stars and the night sky before the air pollution in the city blocked it out. He’s a stocky guy with decent musculature, its plain he works out or at least has enough physical activity he doesn’t have to.

sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: As a netrunner he’s hyperaware, an excellent surveillance, recon, or overwatch provider. Too many years helping facilitate black market deals and bringing down competition for money has earned him some amount of skill with firearms, negotiations, and a handy social skillset for making business partners.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons:
He rarely carries any but he does own a simple handgun. He mostly relies on his ability to fuck with tech as a weapon instead of direct confrontation.
List all weapons, caliber, limitations, etc.
sections.Backstory: Inno grew up in Night City, a middle class son of a Corpo laborer, poor choom never made quite enough to get them out from under Arasaka but was a hard enough worker to be asked for… overnight shifts. He never really knew what happened while his Dad was there but he always came back looking a little worse for wear, and, it just kept going for years. As he grew up he gained a fascination with chrome and netrunning, hearing stories of some hotshot chrome junkie inventing a new virus or hack that could make your toaster rip an exec’s face off, or turn a normal cctv camera into a ticking time bomb from the other side of the city. His dad kicked him out after two years of gig-work when he finally started doing real work, said he couldn’t risk being associated with a cyberpyscho in the making. He started doing any job that came across his path, moving cargo, providing overwatch and logistics for ops, anything. Then came the mods. The exotic implants and sculpting, the paranoia, the access, the weapons, the danger, the fear, the sex, drugs, and shit blowing up in your face, the running for your life just to make a quick eddie. He would make it big, if the fucking toaster would stop calling his neighbor Mrs. Nightly a rogue AI…
sections.RP Hooks: He’s always looking for work, trying to make another score happen or get paid. Doesn’t matter for who or what, a steady gig or a one off money shot, if you need something he’s down to try.

sections.Name: Acier Batallie.
sections.Psychological Profile: A nepo-baby through and through, with an attitude to match. A pathological liar and a high self esteem, they often act aloof and uncaring to most situations but are much sharper than they appear at first. Has a bent towards the sadistic at times.
sections.Physical Description:
Short, thin, feminine. A total Femboy, with scales, horns and a tail for aesthetic purposes.
Chrome / Implants
Arms & Arm Attachments: Cyberarms (Dexterous).
Ocular System: SmartEyes.
Miscellaneous: Ghost Tag. Bio-Monitor.
sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: Stealth, smooth talking, lockpicking, torture, assassination.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: Dagger, Overture Revolver, Bullet Resistant Clothing.
sections.Backstory: Born to two high ranking Arasaka Intel operatives in the Paris branch, they moved to Night City when their parents were redeployed there. Growing up in the life of luxury as befitting an Arasaka nepo-baby made them a very spoiled child, but eventually they were forced by their parents into Arasaka University to ensure they got a proper education. Following in their parent's footsteps upon graduation, they made quick work of establishing a place for themselves. Leveraging their parent's position when able to give themselves a boost in their career, silently removing rivals through various means (typically assassination), and proving they could walk the walk along with the talk. With so much strange activity around East Side in recent months, Arasaka Intel will be deploying Acier to keep an eye on things and report any findings.
sections.RP Hooks: Spying, info-brokering, blackmailing, nepo-baby corpo fun.

sections.Backstory: Born in Santo Domingo, at the time called South Haywood in 2023, Howitzer grew up in the streets of NC, during the time of red after the night city Holocaust, originally as a merc, making a name for herself and working her ass off for it. She joined Millitech and fought in the unification war, solidifying her name sake after a howitzer exploded perminantly damaging her spine, leg and arm leading to her current prosthesis. After that she continued to work as a merc for a few years, spending what amouts of eddies she could save up to keep her in the work force, and looking good while she did it. In recent years she has gotten more intense augments to keep her in fighting form.
sections.RP Hooks: Old school merc with a lot of stories and inventive ways of handling issues. Good for tactical knowhow and logistical shit. Street kid that knows the city like the back lf her hand.

sections.Name: =M+I-R~I^S= (Holo Tag: @The_Doll_Maker)
sections.Occupation: Black market chrome dealer, Underground Ripperdoc, and "Information extraction" for hire.
sections.Psychological Profile: Disassociated, melancholy and quiet, She will do ANYTHING for the right sum. This makes her great at black market dealing and ripperdoc work. She is not a sadist, however doing her job is one of the few things she takes satisfaction in. It is likely she suffers from extreme depression and emotional detachment.
sections.Physical Description: She is an FBT, 2 meters in height, her build is slender and stringy, Her highly exposed internals, such as nerves, tubes, and skeletal structure is her main noticeable feature, She is human, though her appearance makes her look more like a ghost/corpse, She is Generally seen and highly unsettling but dependeble. She has an Infamous record of being impossible to flip/buy out. Once she is hired for something she will not stop until it is done, and cannot be persuaded otherwise, except by the original customer.
sections.Chrome / Implants:
Cyberarms (Dex): Used for surgery, chrome installation. (Hers are known to make unsettling bone cracking and popping sounds)
-Cost: 2

SmartEyes: Thermals, NV, Zoom, etc. (Here eyes look dead, she stares at people for uncomfortable amounts of time, as if she is deconstructing them)
Cost: 2

Cybernetic Claws: INCREDIABLY SHARP, she uses these rather than a scalpel when cutting. (Her claws are always stained slightly with blood)

(I would like to propose having her claws be able to heat themselves, so that she can cauterize wounds)

sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: Non-cyber skills — Stealth, she moves unnaturally quietly, She is good at black market work and business dealings, despite her unnerving presence. HIGHLY talented ripper doc, is able to triage and assist in medical support in dangerous and life threatening situations. She is also a decent shot with her signature Overture, she likes to damage a target as little as possible, damage devalues product after all. Though she prefers others to do the shooting for her.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons:
Malorian Arms .42 caliber Overture
medical supplies, stimulant inhalers, pain suppressants.
sections.Backstory: Miris is an enigma, not even her exact age is certain, she is likely in her 50s (OOC her biological remains are 53). She has been in the underground chrome and ripperdoc culture for the majority of her life. She is responsible for installing illegal chrome in nearly every gang in NC. She holds no allegences and no conections outside of her work. She works purely for profit and everything, from flesh, to emotion, to life and death has stopped mattering to her. When she became an FBC, and why is also unknown, but ask anyone who knows her and they will say they've always remember her as an FBC. She is notorious for being impossible to buy out, once she has been paid, she cannot be flipped or called off, except for by the original customer. Its possible her obsession with using work as a distraction makes her ignore the politics of it, as she just wants to do her job.
sections.Known Affiliations:

sections.Name: Diego Serrano
sections.Psychological Profile: Diego is the kind of person who moves first and thinks while he’s already in motion. But he doesn't freeze nor hesitate, he fully commits. To most people, that looks like recklessness but in reality, it’s experience. He’s been in enough bad situations to know that overthinking gets you killed faster than a wrong decision. He has a strong sense of what’s right and wrong, even if he’d never explain it out loud.He doesn’t like bullies, doesn’t tolerate people abusing power, and won’t stand by if something feels off. That said, he’s not trying to be a hero. He just reacts instinctively. He can be loyal, but only after trust is earned, and that takes time.
sections.Physical Description:
Diego has a solid, athletic build thanks to years of constant movement. He carries himself in a relaxed way, almost casual, even when things are tense, as if he already accepted how things might go.His hair is one of the first things people notice, split between teal, white and black, styled in a messy undercut with longer strands falling forward.
He usually wears tinted glasses. His face is sharp, with light stubble, and there are cyberware lines along his neck and face, nothing flashy, just enough to show he’s been through upgrades.

🦾 Chrome / Implants

Integumentary System:
NanoStitch Skin (2 CWP) – Automatically seals small wounds. Used to counter tech weapons that puncture small but deep holes into soft tissue. Less effective against powered weapons.
Neural:
ICE Layer (1 CWP) – Detects incoming hacks instantly—early warning.
Miscellaneous:
GhostTag (1 CWP) – Your face is automatically blurred in security cameras. Higher chance to be unidentifiable in camera footage.

sheet.preamble:
Nixie
NIX-13
ARASAKA DESIGNATION
ARASAKA
荒坂

sections.Occupation:
High-end Exotic / Joytoy
Covert Arasaka Counter-Intelligence Operative

PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE

Nixie is quiet and shy at first, using synthetic speech, holo-text, and expressive gestures to communicate. Once comfortable, she becomes teasing, affectionate, and playfully mischievous.

She is highly observant and remembers every detail. Beneath her soft demeanor lies a lonely, hypervigilant young woman conditioned by Arasaka who struggles with the fear that her personality is more performance than reality.

TRAUMA:
Lost her voice after a traumatic event in childhood.
Conditioned by Arasaka to use affection as a tool.
Fears abandonment and becoming obsolete.
BEHAVIORAL QUIRKS:
Tail flicks reveal her mood.
Tilts her head when curious.
Uses holo-emojis and cute stickers in conversation.
Remembers small personal details about everyone.
EMOTIONAL TONE:

Warm, playful, and mysterious on the surface; anxious, introspective, and conflicted underneath.

PHYSICAL DESCRIPTION

Petite (5’2”), with a succubus-inspired appearance.
Small decorative horns and bat-like wings, both purely cosmetic. Long black hair with crimson streaks, often worn tousled and wild.

Her glowing red eyes, subtle under-skin lights, and sleek face mask enhance her otherworldly allure. Her movements are graceful, playful, and almost dreamlike—like an illusion you can’t resist.

CHROME / IMPLANTS
AUDITORY SYSTEM (2 CWP)

Enhanced Ears
Enhanced hearing, audio amplification, directional sound filtering.

OCULAR SYSTEM (2 CWP)

SmartEyes
Zoom, threat vision, tactical AR HUD.

CIRCULATORY & IMMUNE SYSTEMS (1 CWP)

Detoxifier
Filters toxins from her bloodstream, protects against drugs and poisons.
Side Effect: Constant thirst; requires frequent hydration.

MISCELLANEOUS (1 CWP)

VoiceWeaver
Synthetic speech, voice modulation, accent mimicry.
Side Effect: Voice can glitch when stressed; may sound artificial.

AESTHETIC CYBERWARE (0 CWP)
Decorative Horns (cosmetic only)
Bat Wings (cosmetic only)
Decorative Tail Mount (cosmetic only)
UnderSkin Lights (red)
Purely aesthetic, no functional benefit.
sections.Total Cyberware Points:
6 / 6
MAX 6 AT CHARACTER CREATION

SKILLS / TALENTS / ABILITIES
Exceptional eavesdropping & intelligence gathering
Reading body language & emotional cues
Seduction & social engineering
Stealth & discreet surveillance
Nonverbal communication
Data broker & information handling
Basic self-defense
EQUIPMENT / ARMOR / WEAPONS
Stylish face mask with holo display support
Designer clothing suited for high-end venues
Agent device & encrypted comms link
Concealed data shards
Pepper spray / small holdout weapon
(if permitted)
BACKSTORY

Born in Night City’s underbelly, Nixie lost her voice after a traumatic event. Arasaka took her in, healed her, and saw potential beyond her silence. Trained as a covert intelligence asset—NIX-13. She now uses charm, playfulness, and her unnatural grace to gather secrets from those who never see the threat behind her smile.

She dreams of discovering who she would be without the mask, the chrome, and the code.

KNOWN AFFILIATIONS

ARASAKA

Various luxury clubs and braindance lounges across Night City.

sections.Name: Echo
sections.Occupation:
Underground DJ, Ripperdoc, Tech Scavenger.
PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Echo comes across as calm and hard to read most of the time, usually speaking in a dry, sarcastic tone that makes it difficult to tell whether he’s joking or insulting someone. He enjoys teasing people during conversations and has a habit of making smart remarks even in dangerous situations.
Underneath the sarcasm, Echo is observant, serious, and far more intelligent than he lets people assume. He tends to study people quietly before deciding whether he trusts them. He dislikes emotional vulnerability and usually hides concern behind teasing comments or deadpan humor.
Echo is obsessed with music and constantly has something playing through his headphones or speakers while he works. He mixes tracks, edits audio, and samples random city sounds into experimental music projects under his alias “ZERO.”
He also has a bad habit of stealing useful tech components whenever he spots them. Wires, chips, processors, broken electronics, audio equipment — if something looks salvageable, there’s a good chance Echo already slipped it into his bag.

PHYSICAL DESCRIPTION
Height: 6’1
Build: Slim and agile

General Appearance:
Echo has layered teal-and-black hair, pale skin, glowing glasses, and rabbit-ear styled headphones that are part of his signature look. He wears oversized black techwear hoodies with dangling straps and utility belts filled with tools, wires, and stolen components.

CHROME / IMPLANTS
Noise Dampener (Auditory System)- Higher chance to filter out harmful sonic frequencies.
Trade off: Temporary deafness if overwhelmed.
Cyberarm (Dexterous)  (Arms & Arm Attachments) - Precision tool use. RP lockpicking, surgery, mechanical hacking.
Trade off: Shakes when under pressure. Doesn't enhance Stamina
Cyberleg (Runner) (Legs & Mobility) - Jump farther, run faster, land silently. Parkour beast.
Trade Off: Loud stomp at full speed. Doesn't enhance Stamina

BACKSTORY
Echo grew up around the underground club scene of Watson, surrounded by loud music, black-market braindances, and scavenged technology. During the day he trained under local ripperdocs, learning how to repair and install cyberware for mercs and gang members.

At Instead of focusing on raw strength or military-grade chrome, Echo trained himself to fight with speed, agility, and unpredictable movement. He relies on fast reflexes, quick strikes, and evasive combat techniques to overwhelm opponents.

Outside of work, Echo constantly scavenges electronics and steals useful tech components to improve his equipment, music gear, and workshop.

sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: Good with knives, petty theft. Due to her childish nature and taking advantage of her birth gender, she manages to worm her way into social groups (sometimes with less success than she realises)
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: A gravity blade (just a switch blade that the handle opens, and when spun. It uses gravity to release the blade)
sections.Backstory:
She never knew, or had any parents. She always knew life on the streets. Her knife skills started with opening packets of food with a rusted blade, to stabbing other street rats who tried to get their dirty mits on her meal. After a couple years, she had gotten pretty good at carvin up runts who tried to steal from her. This got attention from fixers trying to find grunt work some corpo's weren't willing to bother with. This lead to her knife skills becomming second nature.

However due to her life in the dirty streets, eating godknows what. During a checkup she was told she had stomach cancer which had spread through most of her digestive tract. This left her with three options. Die like a pathetic pile of meat to a desease. Medical treatment which she couldn't afford in this life. Or find some sketchy ripperdoc to remove the problematic parts. This lead her to option 3. Replacement.

This lead her to coming across a ripperdoc called selene (agreed upon by mystic_moony) who agreed to do the surgery, but this put her in quite a bit of debt. But it was still cheaper than the alternative. During the proceedure since Selene didn't fully know Lixoriah or trusted the girl to not work off her debt. She had looked around for any chip she had on hand to have atleast some control over her, which ended up being a DollChip. Now on some nights when she doesn't meet her qouta,  Selene uses the DollChip to have her pay... In other ways.

To help with her paying off her debt, if in the job descriptions for the work she recieves doesn't say to keep the body intact, she often ends up cutting out small chrome to stuff in her pocket. Stuff like eyes, fingers and anything else she can stuff in her pockets.
sections.Known Affiliations: Any bums or fixers she can find grunt work for. Like 'handling' a problem. Or hacking into some low-class person's bank account. (a player ripperdoc, not sure who yet)

sections.Backstory: Orphaned alongside his sister Naomi when he was 14, Artyom tried his best to find a way to support himself and his sister. Taking on       simple jobs such as package delivery or cleaning up shops until one day, he returned to his shelter only to find the place was ransacked and his sister was gone. He tried to go to NCPD, but they stated that "People go missing all the time and we can't find them all." He was nearly at his end when he was brought into The Night Watch. Like minded individuals who wanted to help, but like most if all other groups, they wanted help in return. Since then, he has been doing everything in his power to make sure what happened to him doesn't end up the same way.

sections.Additional Notes: Tends to be a bit of a soft heart, Will be hostile to members of Maelstrom and Scavs.

sections.Psychological Profile:
** 
Rafael Alvarez is a friendly but somewhat nervous man outside of combat, often shy around new people until he becomes comfortable. Once relaxed, he’s energetic, enthusiastic, and openly passionate about fighting culture, frequently fanboying over respected fighters, solos, and athletes he admires.
Rafi genuinely loves competition and sees combat as a form of self-improvement rather than cruelty. He actively seeks stronger opponents, believing struggle and adversity are necessary to grow stronger both physically and mentally. In the ring, he’s known for smiling during difficult fights, adapting quickly under pressure, and respecting opponents regardless of victory or defeat.
However, if someone becomes openly disrespectful or forces violence outside agreed competition, his demeanor changes immediately. The excitement disappears, replaced by focused aggression.
sections.Physical Description:
**  
Rafi is a tall, athletic exotic with a lean fighter’s build shaped by years of training rather than heavy cyberware. He has dark green fur with tan striping, bright teal eyes, expressive ears, and prominent horns. His hands are almost always wrapped. He typically dresses in casual athletic clothing like tank tops, hoodies, joggers, and worn sneakers, carrying himself with a relaxed but balanced posture that immediately gives away his combat experience.

sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons:
Prism— A unique, mysterious gold mask passed down from warrior to warrior. Delivers targeting data for long distance shots. Readouts for wind, humidity, range finding, and bullet drop. Has a distinct aggressive will of its own.

Djinn or 恶魔— Custom bodysuit designed by Kang—tao. Provides the wearer with minimal ballistic armor protection, designed mostly to protect from hazardous environments or materials. Blended synthetic weave helps the wearer move quietly, and with dexterous motion. 

Nemesis— 50.Caliber Bolt action sniper rifle. Light weight aluminum body. Chromed 1/12 grooved barrel with a right ward spin. Weighs 25 pounds. Every piece is a custom designed, and meticulously made part. Down to the bolts. (Post character purchase)

Silence— Fiendishly designed suppressed pistol.
sections.Backstory:
Born in old Dubai. Child of street urchins, and scum. Mirage started life, and expected it to be abruptly ended at the end of a gun from early on. 

However in an unfortunate fate she would fall into the clutches of criminal organizations diabolical in their machinations. Trained in the schools of artful long range death. Her life became only that. A whisper followed by the percussion of a bullet impact. Then the ever sweet snap of nothingness. 

She is an expendable tool designed to deliver the shot to change fates. The silent headsman, and maker of the noose.

Her talents were recognized, and was soon adopted into the fold of one of her employers who favored her. The corporate entity known as Kang-tao kept her as a retainer, and employee. A magnanimous reward for a decade of her quiet, discreet, and ever loyal service. 

A mercenary for hire turned corporate Assassin. Mirage hunts down any, and all that she is assigned to eliminate. Firing twice, and then disappearing. 

Her greatest accolade? Joining the Pacifica clean up operation. Eliminating any, and all civilians or gang members who tried to escape. She is now hunting the remnants. One by one.
sections.Known Affiliations:
Kang-tao (BinaryRogue)

RP-Hooks: 
Mirage is a foil. A boogeyman sent by a company to eliminate all targets she can find with extreme prejudice. Her mission is the end of all threats to corporate expansion, and will do anything to achieve it. 

She is also quite skilled, and will make for an excellent long ranged sniper/scout for teams on jobs.

She will however not openly state her intentions as a corporate enforcer. Nor will she be outwardly aggressive to others. She believes in hunting worthy prey, and will only loose her fury on those deserving. Her initial interaction with the players will be more altruistic to gain trust, and gather information for her true prize. The last of the VDB, and their associates.

sections.Name: Dart Kobichenko
sections.Psychological Profile:
Quiet, hard-headed, and fiercely independent. Dart carries himself with an old-school Texan mentality despite growing up in the Nomad packs outside Night City — respect is earned, words matter, and weakness gets people killed.

He prefers simple truths over polished lies and has little patience for corpos, manipulators, or people who hide behind status. Not openly hostile, but difficult to approach.

Dart avoids attachments because most people in Night City either die, betray you, or sell you out for eddies. Despite that, he has a strong protective streak toward Nomads, drifters, and people trying to survive honestly in a dishonest city.
He believes;
A handshake means something.
Never draw unless you intend to kill.
Debt should always be repaid.
Family is chosen, not inherited.
sections.Physical Description:
Tall and lean with a wiry Nomad build shaped more by survival and long-distance travel than gym chrome culture. Dart carries himself with the relaxed posture of someone always ready to move, fight, or leave at a moment’s notice.

He has pale skin lightly weathered by years spent outside Night City’s walls, with long black hair usually tied back into a rough ponytail while loose bangs hang over part of his face. His eyes are a striking icy blue, enhanced with subtle optical cyberware that glows faintly in low light. Thin blue cybernetic interface lines are visible near his right eye and temple.

Dart’s style is practical and understated compared to most Edgerunners. He favors dark clothing, durable fabrics, fitted black jackets or work shirts, tactical belts, and reinforced boots rather than flashy neon fashion. Most people mistake him for a mechanic, drifter, or convoy hand until they notice how observant and armed he really is.

His cyberware is intentionally low-profile. The Monowire systems hidden in his arms remain invisible unless activated, where glowing filament lines briefly become visible beneath the skin.

🦾 Chrome / Implants

sections.Name: Kyatchi "Mirror" Norunobu (Kya - pronounced like Kaya - for short)
sections.Psychological Profile: While not the brightest, she tries to be helpful when she can even if it means getting her hands dirty. She's quick to react, much like an anxious rabbit, but that's one of the main reasons she's so good at what she does. Sometimes, she may be clueless or impulsive (especially when it comes to purchases on useless items she likes such as toy cars or plushies), but other times she can be rather serious - namely when it comes to her work. She isn't good at picking up on social queues but tries to make the best of situations when she can. Being so new to the agency, she hasn't had any super traumatic experiences as of yet but has seen her fair share of awful scenes - especially after a bad crash during her races. She does, however, tend to be slightly distant at first just due to her awkwardness and lack of social cues. To combat this, she's usually listening to her eurobeat playlist in her headphones.
sections.Physical Description: Sitting at a comfortable 4'9", she's...rather short. It doesn't help she lacks any real strength. But what she lacks in strength, she makes up for in dexterity...when her body isn't getting in the way. She's rather blessed in the asset department with a large chest and thighs to balance it out. She has long hair and looks like your typical human, just shorter. She is the definition of a shortstack in this regard.

sections.Name: Lillian Cardinal | also called “Little One”
sections.Psychological Profile:
Lillian is a very quiet woman, having seen a lot of loss and death at the time of her coming into the RP.
Lillian will react badly to death and injuries when it comes to those closest to her. If someone gets heavily injured in front of her or incapacitated, she may freeze or go into a panicked frenzy state… it really depends on what’s happening at the time.
Lillian tends to stare off into space and get quiet when there’s yelling, almost like shutting down emotionally.
She often listens to music as a distraction from her own mental issues.
sections.Physical Description:
Lillian has cybernetics used for netrunning as well as SmartEyes that glow orange.
She has long hair, normally in a braid, with red highlighted ends. 
Lillian has various scars from working with her father and the mercenary group… gunshots, slashes, a small burn or two as well.
She has a few tattoos and will get more done over time as her story progresses… the tattoos showing how far she’s come!
sections.Chrome / Implants: Netrunner Suite lvl 2 (3) ICE Layer (1)  Smart Eyes (2)
sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: Netrunning, Hacking, and Stealth! She’s also a decent cook and likes to make meals for people.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: Aside from her cybernetics, she doesn’t have anything after her stuff got stolen by the other Mercenary Group.
sections.Backstory:
Lillian’s parents were part of a mercenary group when they had Lillian. She was raised with the mercenary group, all of them acting as aunts/uncles to her as she grew up.
Lillian’s mom died during one of the jobs when Lillian was about 16 years old.
Lillian’s dad stayed with the mercenary group, ensuring Lillian was safe and learned how to netrun and defend herself, even if she doesn’t use a gun.
Now roughly 25, Lillian, her dad, and the mercenary group went on a job and ran into another mercenary group, and a fight broke out. Both sides faced losses, but Lillian’s father died trying to protect Lillian and get her away after she’d been injured. The rival mercenary group took Lillian and her father’s items, assuming Lillian was dead after the fight.
Injured, Lillian played dead until they were gone, ripping her shirt to use as a way to stop the bleeding of her wounds until she could find something to crudely stitch herself up.
Now she wanders, lost in the cloud of dread surrounding her thoughts.

sections.Backstory:
Hailing from Arizona with the Chenery Clan, a small band of close knit Nomads, Nicole grew up with a loving family, travelling across the lands, doing odd jobs here and there and learning vital survival skills along the way.
At one point, several raids from Raffen Shivs had diminished the Chenery Clan’s strength and the conversation of joining one of the Seven Nomad Nations cropped up, the younger, more stubborn members flatly refused, Nicole amongst them.
During one of their jobs at Illinois, the Chenery’s elected to help the Jodes with the reconstruction of Chicago, repairing a few of their locomotives. Many members believed in The Jodes’ plan of reconstruction and decided to join up with them. 
Nicole felt like this was abandoning the clan’s identity and refused, electing to go her own way and drifted back West, at one point even being captured by Raffen Shivs only being freed by Aldecaldos attacking the compound, allowing her to escape and eventually make her way to Night City.
sections.RP Hooks: Raffen Shivs might come along one day, survivors from the compound attack and might recognize her and her car, wanting to reclaim what they see as rightfully theirs.

I have spoken to both <@122199279447048192> | Toro and @Vinny Russo/Vanessa Bitch and both have agreed to this happening down the line. For now, I'm playing him normally as a Valentino, getting him engrained with everyone, getting to know people with him so they learn what kind of person he is and make some sort of attachment to him, so that when the Maelstrom bit happens, it'll hit harder and show players just how awful Maelstrom can really be.

sections.Occupation:
Freelance Courier & Mechanic
Location: Santo Domingo, Night City

Psychological Profile
Kael is quiet, cautious, and emotionally guarded. He keeps conversations short unless he trusts someone and prefers to avoid attention whenever possible. He is not ambitious in the traditional Night City sense — he is not trying to become a legendary mercenary or climb corporate ranks. His focus is survival.
Years of unstable work, debt, and stress have left him exhausted and detached. Despite this, he still has a difficult time ignoring people in trouble, even when helping them creates more problems for himself.
He handles pressure unusually well, often becoming calmer the worse a situation gets. Unfortunately, that same mindset causes him to bottle up stress until he eventually disappears for days at a time.
His biggest issue is money. Kael constantly borrows from dangerous people, convinced each job will finally let him catch up.

Physical Description
Height: 5’10”
Lean build
Messy long black hair, usually tied loosely back
Brown eyes with cheap amber-tinted cyberoptics
Visible exhaustion around the eyes from poor sleep
Faint cybernetic lines near the neck and temples
Usually seen in dark hoodies, worn bomber jackets, cargo pants, gloves, and combat boots
Overall appearance:
A tired Night City courier who blends into crowds until you look closely.

Cyberware & Implants
Neural
NeuroPort Interface Plug
Basic shard access and device interfacing.
Tradeoff: Frequent headaches after heavy use.
Ocular System
Budget Kiroshi Optics
Low-light vision and basic zoom functions.
Tradeoff: Minor visual static and delayed focus occasionally.
Hands & Feet
Sub-Dermal Grips
Improved grip strength and handling.
Tradeoff: Reduced sensitivity in fingertips.
Miscellaneous

3 / 6

Skills & Talents
Motorcycle repair
City navigation
Handgun handling
Basic lock bypassing
Fast driving
Street negotiation
Route memorization
Basic first aid

Equipment
Unity 9mm pistol
Combat knife
Light armored jacket
Portable mechanic toolkit
Old Yaiba motorcycle
Burner phones
Disposable data shards

Backstory
Kael grew up in Santo Domingo surrounded by factories, rolling blackouts, and gang violence. His mother worked maintenance jobs while he spent most of his teenage years running errands and small deliveries to help keep food on the table.
He stayed independent rather than joining a gang or corporation, building a reputation as someone reliable enough to trust with deliveries but quiet enough not to ask questions.
A failed courier job a few years ago left him in serious debt after valuable cargo disappeared during a firefight between local boosters and private security. Kael survived, but the people expecting payment did not care whose fault it was.
Since then, he has spent most of his time taking increasingly risky jobs trying to stay ahead of what he owes. Every month he gets a little closer to paying it off — and every month something else goes wrong.
Kael is not famous, dangerous, or important. He is simply another Night City resident trying not to drown under the weight of the city.

Known Affiliations
Independent courier circles
Local mechanics
Small fixer contracts

sections.Name: Specter (Real Name robert Locket but keeps it secret)
sections.Psychological Profile: Specter is defined by Survivors Guilt and Cold Pragmatism. He is generally quiet, preferring the company of machines to people. He hates the cold (ironic given his design) and seeks warmth constantly. He has a hatred for "sloppy code" and acts of hubris. While he tries to be a "ghost" and stay unnoticed, he has a soft spot for the "broken things" of the world—be they machines or people.

sections.Backstory:
Aurora grew up as an only child in a small town south of Night City where she had a pretty decent childhood. She wasn't sure if that was due to the fact that her parents were simply trying to make it up to her for not being around the house as often as they used to be when she was younger due to their jobs as pharmaceutical researchers for BioTechnica. 

The older she got it seemed that they were around a whole lot less. Really throwing themselves into their work and always giving the excuse that they were working so much to try and secure her a future job at the same branch of BioTechnica they worked for. 

When she hit eighteen that's when things really changed for Aurora. Sure she had worked odd jobs when she was a bit younger like working at any restaurants or clubs in her city that would hire someone on the younger side. Because she needed to fend for herself most of the time. 

When she turned eighteen. She started to dabble in starting to sell brain dances because it helped her make Eddies. But she also started to dance at the clubs that she had used to serve food for. 

Unfortunately, even though she felt as if her parents were purposefully distancing themselves from her as she got older. Once they found out what kind of profession she had they were livid. 

It resulted in a rather heated argument between the three of them. They went with the same old excuse of how they were working so hard in order to give her a good life. And how doing what she was doing was just throwing her life away. 

So, after the argument she instantly went to her room and started to pack up some of her essentials. 

She didn't have a whole lot of money saved up. But she prayed that she could at least make it to the bigger city. 

Aurora had a feeling that if she got there. She would be a lot better off. 

Upon her arrival to Night City. She had managed to snag a job as a Brain Dance peddler once more as well as being a Joy Toy/Dancer as a side gig.

sections.Name: *** Elias “Delta” Kade
sections.Psychological Profile: *** Officer Elias “Delta” Kade demonstrates strong psychological resilience and disciplined emotional control under extreme stress. He is a tactical thinker who remains calm in chaotic situations, often prioritizing the safety of fellow officers and civilians over personal risk. While the Watson depot incident left him with lingering survivor’s guilt, it strengthened his determination to lead responsibly and prevent similar losses. Delta shows a deep intolerance for corruption and reckless policing, preferring controlled, strategic responses over unnecessary force. His leadership style is protective, grounded, and dependable, making him well suited for maintaining stability in volatile environments like BeastSide.
sections.Physical Description: *** Elias “Delta” Kade stands with a solid, athletic build shaped by years of tactical training and active duty within the NCPD. His posture is disciplined and alert, reflecting someone accustomed to operating in high-risk environments. Delta carries himself with a calm, controlled presence, often scanning his surroundings out of habit. Subtle scars and wear from past engagements mark his experience in the field, while his movements remain precise and efficient. His overall appearance gives the impression of a seasoned officer, someone hardened by Night City but still composed enough to lead when situations turn chaotic. It was the only thing that saved him before coming to Beast District.

When she got older she started working with the yakuza as a netrunner to save up enough eddies to get a full body conversion. She took what remained of her money and smuggled herself to night city to escape assassination by the hands of the yakuza in Japan. Fleeing one hell to enter another.

sections.Backstory:
Astrid came from money, and showed some extreme promise in the field of Netrunning. Arasaka actually scouted her from a fairly early age, and she managed to get herself fast-tracked into higher education to, eventually, end up as a proper Arasaka Netrunner agent. 
A combination of laziness, overconfidence and general richkid syndrome had them fall behind fairly quickly in their studies. In an attempt to keep ahead, she successfully managed to cheat her way through several major tests, and ended up at the top of her class. Life was pretty good.
Being not actually that good, though, eventually catches up with you.
She eventually got caught attempting to penetrate Arasaka servers to adjust some of her grades to keep herself on top - and in doing so, prompted an investigation into her entire academic career.
Needless to say, she was expelled in shame. Her family essentially disowned her, and she used most of what little cred she had to get herself a small apartment for a couple of months. And, like so many others who need a fresh start, she ended up in Night City.
All thats left now is to get a damn job and hopefully not get scav’d for her extremely nice Arasaka chrome - which she, at the very least, managed to not ping as Arasaka tech. Still, best not to let anybody not trustworthy get too close a look.
sections.RP Hooks:
-Ex Arasaka Student
-Has never held a gun in her life
-Carries Arasaka implants
-Nebulously in debt to keep herself alive until she manages to get a job.
-Probably hacking things to  get herself enough scratch to keep herself off the street.
sections.Additional Notes:
I built this character to intentionally be a little barebones, so that I could build them into something more fitting into the setting - or build a different character once I was more comfortable with the RP. I figure the best way to get involved and learn the ropes it to play a character that also has no idea what the hell they’re doing.
ADjusted backstory - Changed stuff to Arasaka, removed allusions to leaving the country, and switched the handle/alias to something much easier to just say in passing.

sections.Name: Ronan Vance - "Stray"
sections.Physical Description: A hyenid/jackal-class exotic with sharper features and an athletic build
sections.Chrome / Implants:
Circulatory & Immune Systems - Chemical Analyzer
Miscellaneous - ICE Layer
Neural - Netrunner Suite (Level 3)
Ocular System - Smart Eyes
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: Militech Lexington, Militech Ajax, Netrunning Suit
sections.Backstory:
Ronan  grew up in Night City, learning early that survival depended on information as much as firepower. While others kicked down doors, Ronan found his niche behind a deck—slipping into security systems, looping cameras, cracking locks that weren’t meant to be opened. For a while he ran with a small crew that called themselves a pack: smugglers, runners, and street mercs scraping out a living job by job. Ronan handled the Net, keeping eyes on the grid while the rest handled the physical side of things.

It worked for a while... until it didn’t.

The jobs got bigger, the risks got worse, and eventually the pack fell apart. Some disappeared into the city, others didn’t make it out of a job alive. Ronan walked away with a working deck, a handful of scars, and the realization that in Night City, loyalty only goes so far before survival takes over.

The exotic mods came later.

At first it was practical—biosculpting and cyberware that made him harder to overlook and harder to intimidate. But the changes kept going. Digitigrade legs, reinforced claws, enhanced senses—the kind of modifications that give him a presence most runners don’t have. The result is something unusual in the city: a netrunner who looks like he belongs on the front line instead of hiding behind a chair in some safehouse.

Most runners stay off-site. Stray runs in the field.

His handle started as a joke from another runner—someone noticing he never stuck with a crew for long. The name stuck. Ronan didn’t bother changing it. These days it fits too well.

Now he works the streets of Night City as an independent operator. Sometimes that means cracking corporate security for a merc crew. Sometimes it means ghosting through a system so a fixer’s job goes smoothly. He’s done work for all kinds—mercs, clubs, the occasional gang that needs someone who can break into a network without asking too many questions. He keeps his circle small and his alliances temporary.

Ronan carries himself with a quiet confidence—cocky enough to know he’s good at what he does, but smart enough to remember the city is full of people who’d love to prove him wrong. In the Net, his presence is subtle but unmistakable: systems failing quietly, access doors opening at the right moment, cameras going dark just long enough for someone to slip through.

sections.Backstory:
Originally from Toronto Canada, Curtis would spend most of his life tinkering, even as a child he'd mess with whatever sparked his fancy, quickly finding a special place for explosive due to him uncovering an old stash of fireworks. He was by himself for a long time after his mom left and dad was killed but he managed well on his own with help, when his height and shortcomings came into focus as he got older, he'd find more of a interest in Cyberware, then he modified himself to fix his weak arms and hands. With this first taste, he'd go a bit wilder than most, adding in a few extra mods made him break, thankfully, just right to develop a tick that made him more abrasive and aggressive when angered.

Overtime this mixed with a mild Delirium from his mods, he'd start to get into his head that he needed to help people, and would often end up breaking into nearby homes to upgrade things, that sometimes went well, or blew up in his or his neighbor's face. He'd rack up a number of property damage charges, but would always manage to dodge getting booked, until one day after an intense argument with a rather noisy neighbor, he'd bomb their bathroom in a rage, but knowing he'd not have many chances left, he decided to leave Canada.

Before he was as gonked out as he was, he had gotten to know a friend who called herself Blue on an Anime forum over the Net, and had at one point learned that she had planned on living in Night city, so in a mix of  cyberware confusion and a vague recollection of that fact, he left to go find her to crash for a while, but since he didn't tell her about it, and didn't know where she lived, he got himself lost in Pacifica for a time only to finally stumble his way to Eastside trying to find her.

sections.Name: Saburō Shigeru Ryuu
sections.Occupation:
Former Arasaka close-combat instructor → Traditional melee specialist & contract combat advisor

🧠 Psychological Profile
Saburō is disciplined, reserved, and deeply traditional, shaped by corporate Japan’s rigid hierarchy and an old warrior mindset. He values restraint, respect, and efficiency, but he is not naïve—he understands power, obedience, and survival.
Night City represents a cultural shock: chaotic, loud, disrespectful, and violently informal. Rather than reject it outright, Saburō consciously studies the city’s social codes, slang, and unspoken rules, knowing adaptation is the only way to survive without losing himself entirely.
He suppresses emotion in public, showing little reaction even under provocation. Internally, however, he constantly evaluates whether his principles are liabilities or assets in this new environment.
Behavior quirks:
Observes before speaking


Avoids unnecessary confrontation, but never backs down once engaged

sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: Old Barghest jacket and tactical vest, Militech Achilles, Tsunami Nue
sections.Backstory:
Jonah's first memories were of a Nomad convoy cutting through the Badlands—engines humming, dust clouds trailing behind family rigs, voices crackling over shortwave. The clan wasn’t rich, but they were tight. Everyone had a role. Everyone mattered. Survival wasn’t individual; it was shared.

That belief didn’t survive Night City.

The clan took a corporate transport contract they couldn’t afford to refuse. Sealed cargo, armed escort, no questions. Halfway through the route, the deal collapsed. Corporate forces reclassified the convoy as hostile, citing a breach that never existed. The clan was wiped out in the chaos. Those who lived learned fast: the corps needed scapegoats, not partners.

He was one of the few survivors.

Alone, he drifted toward the city’s worst gravity well—Dogtown. Hunger made decisions simple. Morality was a luxury he no longer had.

Barghest found him there.

They didn’t care where he came from. They cared that he could take orders, hold a weapon steady, and keep his mouth shut. They offered food, training, and structure in exchange for obedience. For someone who had lost everything, it felt like purpose.

Barghest stripped him down and rebuilt him.

Urban combat drills. Crowd suppression. Close-quarters dominance. Fire discipline in zones where civilians blurred into acceptable losses. They replaced Nomad loyalty with doctrine, family with unit cohesion, questions with commands.

And it worked.

He became reliable. Efficient. Dangerous.

Years later, he was attached to a quiet operation in Pacifica—a blacksite extraction overseen by unmarked corporate observers. The mission brief was familiar: secure the asset, eliminate witnesses, leave nothing behind.

It was a netrunner. Young. Terrified. Plugged into something that connected megacorps, Dogtown, and black-budget operations in ways no one wanted exposed.

Something in him broke through the conditioning.

He hesitated.

That hesitation sealed his fate.

The kill order updated mid-mission—this time, on him. His squad pulled out without argument. The blacksite was firebombed. Internal records marked him as operationally unreliable. Officially deceased.

He survived anyway.

Burned chrome. Scar tissue. No clan. No unit. No flag left to stand under.

Now he works as a freelance Solo in Night City. He carries Nomad instincts—watching his crew, honoring deals, protecting those who don’t deserve to be crushed. But beneath that runs Barghest steel: discipline, efficiency, and a ruthless understanding of how violence is organized.

He doesn’t trust corporations.
He doesn’t believe in blind loyalty.
And he knows exactly how quickly “family” becomes expendable.

If he chooses to fight beside you, it’s not because of contracts or colors.

It’s because he decided you were worth not leaving behind.

sections.Backstory:
Ayami Sakurai was born in Belton Texas, the youngest of two older brothers, with parents who tried to keep order in a lively household. She often felt overlooked, restless under the weight of expectations. Her mother’s extended family, the Uchiumis, offered a rare connection. Her cousin Jurian, though not technically her full cousin, visited frequently. To Ayami, he was the only one who mattered. With him, she could be loud, untamed, and unconcerned with rules.

From a young age, she carried an insatiable hunger, not just for food, though she could eat two to five large fish a day but for freedom and space to act on her impulses. Growing up, her energy clashed with the rigid rhythm of small-town life. She loved manga, losing herself in its pages for hours, and video games that let her escape. She disliked large dogs, melodrama, and anyone who scorned fishing, small rules she lived by to protect her peace.

Bullies tested her limits. Six boys once cornered her alone, she fought, but she couldn’t win in sheer numbers. After that, she learned to disappear. She could slip into bushes, crouch under stairwells, or hide in quiet corners for hours until danger passed. She became patient.

Her cousin was away during those moments, but help came in unexpected ways. The Ryudo Family noticed her and helped her get away a few times. She admired their loyalty and discipline, even if she rejected their criminal ties. Their world fascinated her. She carried her own joys quietly the taste of fresh sushi, the rhythm of casting a line into water, and the rare moments when she could curl up with manga.

Backstory Continued: After moving to Night City with Jurian, Ayami found that being an exotic made everyday work harder. Her bunny ears drew attention she didn’t want — comments, stares, assumptions. Meatspace demanded conformity she had never liked. So she looked for work that let her stay unseen.

She started small: organizing data shards, tagging archives for others, cleaning corrupted files, mapping virtu's  for people who didn’t have the patience to do it themselves.

These weren’t glamorous jobs, but they paid, and more importantly, they kept her out of the spotlight.

Over time, she learned how data flowed. How security thought. Where systems assumed no one would bother looking. She wasn’t reckless enough to brute-force anything — that wasn’t her nature. Instead, she slipped through gaps the same way she once slipped into bushes or under stairwells. Slowly, carefully, she crossed the line from data handler to netrunner.

sections.Name: *Vox Machina*
sections.Psychological Profile:
*Vox is a volatile but deeply empathetic individual whose identity is built on defiance, self-expression, and survival. He thrives on intensity—loud environments, emotional extremes, confrontation—and uses performance as both armor and therapy. Outwardly abrasive and provocative, he uses his voice to challenge authority and protect those he sees as vulnerable, even if he refuses to admit it. His distrust of institutions and hypersensitivity to control or silence stem from a history of instability and unresolved trauma.*

*Despite his chaotic exterior, Vox is driven by a fierce moral streak and a need to stay visible, relevant, and unrestrained. He spirals when isolated, struggles with emotional regulation, and often sabotages himself through impulsivity. Yet in high-pressure situations, he becomes sharply focused and surprisingly loyal, grounding his identity in the community he builds around him. Vox is not inherently dangerous—only reactive, and only to those who try to cage him.*

sections.Backstory:
**  
* An heiress to a lofty Arasaka seat, Shinohara Risa has lived a life of luxury up until the day she was cut off from her family’s legacy. She would excel in classes, at work events, but then spent the rest of her time scrolling. Despite the many opportunities her family had prepared, Risa often showed malicious incompetence to get out of work. Squandering funds, projects unfinished, and lazing about, it was commonplace to think of Risa as nothing more than a future trophy wife. Less people bothering her while she focused on her own interests. Wanting to find something to put her heart and soul into, but flitting out of an idea moments after committing. The young lady became prone to bouts of programmed perfectionism. The family was rife with discontent, and something had to be done before Arasaka forced a bad hand. Risa was their only child after all, and despite her shortcomings they loved her dearly.

* On a fateful evening, Miss Shinohara would be accosted during an outing with a friend. Her travel plans and security detail leaked to the letter. She would be held for ransom, either monetarily or for information, and her parents would refuse to pay, citing whatever was needed to crush her spirit, and leaving her at the whims of cyberpunks and their fixer. An unspoken invisible order that would be painfully driven: if she would be the one to inherit the family seat, then Risa would return to them under her own power with something to better their position in the corporation. For the sake of the family. For a bright future together.

Lonely, driven, strained. Few genuine connections; truth is her fuel.

sections.Backstory:
Vanessa Belmonte grew up on the Night City docks, daughter of a laid-off longshoreman. From a young age she learned survival meant listening, playing dumb, and collecting secrets. In her late teens she hustled into Network News 54, briefly appearing on-screen before vanishing from the public eye. She began publishing under the byline “Silvia Knox,” exposing corpo smuggling, bribery, and gang deals.

To protect herself, Vanessa underwent exotic sharklike surgery, erasing her old face. Now only a few higher-ups at N54 know Silvia’s true identity. To everyone else, Silvia Knox is just a ghostwriter with no face.

Within Tide, Vanessa plays the role of a bubbly, ditzy exotic—harmless, underestimated, perfect cover. They think she’s comic relief, but every secret spilled around her finds its way into a Silvia Knox expose. She’s underestimated until the moment she draws her Desert Eagle, handling its brutal recoil with unnerving control.

At 25, Vanessa lives two lives: Vanessa, the harmless Tide hanger-on, and Silvia Knox, the feared underground reporter. If either mask slips, she’s dead—but until then, she thrives in the danger, smiling with teeth just a little too sharp.
sections.RP Hooks:
- A corp fixer hunts for “Silvia Knox,” unaware she’s Vanessa.  
- A Tide member starts noticing she’s sharper than she pretends.  
- An old N54 coworker recognizes her voice after surgery.  
- A Silvia Knox story gets too close to Tide’s operations.
sections.Additional Notes:
- She is not explicitly interested in just Tide. Any gang or corpo info is juicy to her. 
- Being from N54, she explicitly stays away from Militech, as her higher ups allow her to continue publishing as long as it does not affect the megacorp.
- Has no real friends
- Constantly questions who she really is
- In some cases, she cares too much
- N54 gives her money to keep up the appearance of her doing Joytoy work, but she rarely ever actually does so

sections.Backstory:
Born and raised a nomad in a small unimportant tribe, grew up with their mother as a key figure in their life. Their mother was the only exotic in the tribe who also carried the appearance of a coyote. His mother passed when he was near the end of his teenage years, roughly 18 years old.
His mother's passing spurred a rebelious streak in Skie against his tribe in wanting to follow his mother's footsteps; becoming an exotic. And this drove him to leave the tribe and make his way to Night City, set on proving them wrong by prospering and sending back money to help them survive.
When he first arrived in Night City he bounced around through different odd jobs; mechanic work, labour, and anything to keep his head above water. During one such a job, covering for the previous bartender of The Den, he met the owner of the bar. (Buddy)
Buddy took an interest in the temporary bartender and through various conversations he made a deal with Skie, offering a contract to cover the cost of his operation to become an exotic. Being overzealous and excited, Skie signed without reading it through.
Signing the contract would lead to his indentured service to "Buddy", acting as his personal Doll, leading to his experience in serving guests.
Skie, ever the optimist, after an offer from "Buddy" to sell Skie the bar on a line of credit which he accepted sight-unseen, as he figured that he could use the profits from the bar to pay off both sets of debt that he owed.

sections.Backstory:
Born and raised in the shadow of the El Coyote Cojo, Joshua Reyes was Valentino royalty in the making. By nineteen, he was a "Soldado" known for two things: a silver tongue that could de-escalate a Militech standoff and a relentless devotion to the neighborhood. He wasn't just a gunman; he was a fixer for the people, ensuring the local vendors weren't squeezed too hard by outside interests. To the Valentinos, Joshua believed that he was a great fit for the gang as he was a man who understood that loyalty to Santa Muerte was measured in blood and brotherhood. 

Everything changed during the Unification War. When the NUSA pushed its recruitment quotas into the independent territories, Joshua found himself caught in a sweep. To protect his younger brother from being taken, Joshua "volunteered," trading his gold-trimmed street leathers for the drab, tactical nylon of the NUSA Army. He was shipped to the scorched outskirts of the Free States, serving as a frontline infantryman. For five years, Joshua saw the ugly side of "unification." He traded urban skirmishes for drone-choked trenches and chemical storms. During the final months of his tour, his unit was tasked to breach and clear out an area within the Alaskian state. When breaching and clearing what should have been an abandoned site, a shotgun took his right arm, leaving his squad to neutralize the threat inside while bleeding out on the cold ground.

Joshua returned to Night City two months ago, not as a hero, but as a "reintegrated veteran" with a discharge paper and a cheap, government issued prosthetic that jitters when the humidity rises. But the Heywood he left isn't the one that he once grew up in. Some of the Valentinos he once knew have shifted. Some new, younger leaders see him as "federalized" or a "Militech puppet" because of his time in the NUSA greens. His right arm, once used to grip the hands of his brothers in solidarity, is now a literal cold reminder of his absence, which has him wondering if he can ever find his way back into what gave him a purpose in this city.

sections.Backstory:
Connor Cestus, a talented fighter raised in a nomad clan, becomes a rising star in Night City’s pro fighting scene. He’s approached by secretive fixers to throw a match so they can keep another fighter profitable, and he agrees for the money. But during the fight, his pride takes over, and he wins instead.

Angered, the fixers demand repayment—an amount Connor can’t afford. To protect his clan, he flees to Night City and disappears, living behind a mask. He survives by fighting in underground matches and street racing, trying to earn money and track down the fixer chasing him so he can settle the debt and reclaim his life.
sections.RP Hooks: he’s abit mysterious but talented loves fighting and racing.

sections.Name: Harry Lunaire
sections.Psychological Profile: Laid back, calm. Generally apathetic to the state of the world. Far more interested in people than in politics. Likes to find out what makes others tick. Very intense when one-on-one, otherwise near impassive. Vastly more interested in living discreetly and privately than pushing any major ambitions.
sections.Physical Description: Tall, slender, nothing more than the most basic of chrome, stylish but not particularly flashy clothing, regular human.
sections.Chrome / Implants: Essentially, the equivalent of a smartphone installation in his head and neck. The basic implants someone needs to interface with Night City technology.
sections.Total Cyberware Points: Couldn’t see this basic setup listed anywhere, so I guess 0.
sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities: He can do his job reasonably well and has a way of smoothing things over with corporate clients. He cooks great ramen. His dad taught him to fire a pistol at one stage. He has a good grasp of mechanics from his father’s tuition and is an enthusiastic but still far from professional roboticist.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: He has a basic 9mm Glock equivalent for self-defence.
sections.Backstory: Harry comes from a family that was torn apart by gangs and gang violence. The child of a mechanic, he was estranged from his mother due to a burglary at a young age. Whilst he now works for a corp, he doesn’t really consider himself a corpo. He simply prioritises staying “safe” as best that he can. He was drawn to robotics through his studies, but he was never quite good enough or expensively educated enough to make the cut on the engineering floor. He settles instead for a client-facing position where he works as best he can with the hope of one day being able to score a sponsorship with which to complete his studies.
sections.Reference Images: Still WIP, trying to get this in as I’ve been told there will be a cut-off soon. The avatar is being built, though!
sections.RP Hooks: Malleability: I seek to provide a relative grounding or normalcy in a setting that is profuse with mad stories and characters. He is very disenfranchised, and the goal will be to motivate him through the stories and connections he makes with others. He’s very interested in why other people think how they think and do what they do. I very much like making people who are malleable and react and grow based on the story that naturally unfolds.

Not much is known about his childhood, only that he was a decent student with a penchant for violence. After his first contract, Marcus got his Bachelors in surgery, going through OCS to become a Mustang Officer. He then spent the rest of his career being good to his soldiers and protecting them from idiotic commanding officers. Near the end of his career, he had a far younger officer, a Colonel named Asha, and had some respect for her. But he hit his 30 year mark and retired, spending the next 20 years working at hospitals across the NUSA, now making his way into night city find the thrill he had had in the Army.

sections.Name: Lucio “La Garra” Cortez
sections.Psychological Profile:
Beware the old who thrive in a young man’s game–Lucio is a consummate professional shrouded in the guise of an older, chilled man; a prize for those he is loyal to, and a staunch enemy to those who they deem a problem. His allegiance is as simple as his word–if you are his people, his very existence is at your disposal; indeed, to be half-hearted in what matters to you is as much an insult to your own person as those around you.

    Much has changed around him, and at times he feels as though he is having difficulty adapting–after all, the chrome is sleeker, and the faces smoother than the halcyon days of his youth; but, with experience comes wisdom, and one ought not mistake a meager acknowledgement of the state of the world around him with resignation. He knows he still has a few tricks up his sleeve yet, and many more to learn.
sections.Physical Description: A perennially imposing,  6’5” frame veiled by age’s softening touch and a taste for junk food, Lucio is a striking image of his exotic-work–a burly black-bear pock-marked with scars and old, paved-over cyberwear.

sections.Backstory:
For years, Lucio “La Garra” Cortez was a furtive whisper in the ears of those who’d see the Valentinos lesser–a hitman of preternatural determination and steely will whose inclination for spectacular executions earned him pride of place among the variegated legends of 2050s and early 2060s Heywood. His origins, unremarkable–the middle son of a large, extended family resident to Heywood’s poorer parts, saw the inchoate sicario exploiting his ample frame as muscle to back-up the district gangs’ own issues.
    
    Jobs supplanting and building, as they do, on one’s reputation, Lucio’s work shifted from simple shakedowns and smuggling to surreptitious hits demanded by the Valentinos’ most illusory–work which the fledgling sicario was all-too-happy to sew, seeing the gang as his sole support network in a world of backstabbers. The eddies rolling in as they did, and his reputation growing increasingly broad and grim among the underbelly of Heywood, Lucio saw to supplant his broad but plain visage with that of a fierce black-bear–an imposing image of strength and territory befitting the sundry nature of his loyalties and work.

    But, as all fortunes fade, and all legends are choked to gasped breaths by father time, so too did Lucio’s ambition and, indeed, legend, get cut down in its prime–the ardours of the Molto Cielo bloodbath, a harrowing coup against Campo Orta in the early 2060s, demanded retribution. La Garra was to be its grim executor, in an act which would ultimately see the sicario in jail for a lengthy fifteen year sentence. Tasked from on-high to dispose of the coup’s twin masterminds, Lucio was caught and–following a protracted legal battle under the tending of lawyer Jesus Costazeulla–Lucio was consigned to a ten year stint in prison. A sentence promptly extended by five years after his execution of a sixth-streeter following the onset of the Valentino/Sixth-Street conflict.

    Now, with no parole, no remaining home, and no eddies, Lucio is at last free from the confines of prison–intending to make up for lost time, with change.

sections.Name:
Vora
Alias: "Hollow-Point" / "The Iron Maiden" / "The Siren of Steel"
sections.Occupation:
Maelstrom Ritualist & Heavy Ordnance Specialist
Affiliation: Maelstrom (Totentanz Chapter)
I. PSYCHOLOGICAL PROFILE
Personality: Sultry, obsessive, and dangerously captivating. Vora views her body as a masterpiece in progress, a "sensory altar" built from cold iron and raw nerve endings. She is a profound masochist who finds a dark, erotic charge in the "ignition spark" of combat. She doesn't just fight; she seduces her environment and her enemies into a dance of mutual destruction, thriving on the edge of system failure to feel the "heat" of her own existence.
Trauma: The H12 Fracture. Vora’s sanity was permanently recalibrated at the age of 3 in Mega Building H12. She witnessed her drug-addicted father murder her mother before she even had a firm grasp on language. Because the trauma occurred during her most formative years, she has no concept of a "normal" or "safe" world. Her older sister Selene (then 10) became her surrogate mother, protector, and eventually, her god. To Vora, the searing pain of Selene’s surgical lasers is the only "caress" she has ever known, leading to a deep, pathological association between agony and maternal security.
Behavioral Quirks: Vora possesses a hair-trigger temper; she reacts to minor technological glitches or verbal slights with sudden, industrial-scale violence. Yet, when she encounters a target she finds "intriguing," her rage cools into a magnetic, hungry presence. She becomes a playful but lethal stalker, "wooing" her chosen prey with agonizing slowness and deliberately exposing her own weaknesses to invite them to strike her—relishing the sensory feedback of their desperate struggle before the final, breathless violation.
Emotional Tone: Sultry and digitized. Her voice is a low, purring reverb that sounds like silk sliding over gravel. When discussing her sister Selene or her "upgrades," her tone shifts into a heavy, breathless reverence that borders on the ecstatic.
II. PHYSICAL DESCRIPTION
Height: 7' 9" | Build: Heavily armored, industrial, and imposing.
Exotic Features: Massive obsidian-colored horns (glowing red tips), digitigrade "talon" feet, with a cyber spine (No function yet)

General Appearance: Matte-black and crimson plating. Fiber-optic silver-white "hair" cables that pulse with "Infernal" red light when she enters combat mode.
Distinguishing Marks: The flesh hidden beneath her external plating is a dense, intricate topography of Selene's "affection." It is covered in perfectly symmetrical surgical scars, ritualistic keloids, and fine-line laser brands that map out every incision ever made by her sister. These marks are often still tender or inflamed, as Vora intentionally irritates them to

sections.Name: Needle
sections.Psychological Profile:
Shy and Soft
Has seen the slums of Australia and survived.
**Needle’s Personality:** Perhaps soft isn’t the right word. She’s still definitely shy, oftentimes very reserved. Growing up on the streets taught her to be untrusting and cautious. Skills that allowed her to survive until TASR picked her off the streets and put a medkit in her hands. Her relationship with the members of TASR was strictly professional. She was just a cog that helped things move smoothly and in exchange she was given food and lodging. The only person she had any kind of closeness with was her overseer, Derry Harris, the head ripper doc of TASR. While still strictly a professional relationship Needle still respected him. Unfortunately, he along with everyone else from TASR, was killed in the assault from the Jackels. 
To add onto this Needle comes off as a bit of a pushover. Taught to not cause any issues or she’d be back on the streets without resources. She was mostly kept away from any fighting during her time as a member of TASR. Though she still saw value in seeking out basic melee practice and was taught by her overseer to load and fire a gun. But beyond tumbling with a few street kids and a few tense moments with other gangs she was mostly kept isolated from violence. Her skills as a ripper doc keeping her off the front lines of any turf disputes or smuggling missions. As a gang member she’s “soft” in the sense she's weak physically compared to other war hardened members.

sections.Backstory:
She was born in Night City, in the district of Kabuki. Her parents were small time hustlers. Not powerful, not important just visible enough to get stepped on. When they died over debt with the Tyger Claw and nobody investigated. Nobody cared. She learned early if you can’t rely on people, you rely on movement. She started parkour rooftops running, climbing, slipping through alleys and scaffolding. Parkour wasn’t just a skill it was survival. It kept her ahead of gangs, cops, and anyone who thought a lone girl was easy prey. On one of her days on the roof tops she lost a arm and legs parkouring falling off a roof and lost all her money getting a new arm and legs
As Maelstrom tightened their grip on Watson, Kabuki changed. The streets got meaner, the chrome got heavier, and the cost of staying independent went up. So she made the call most street kids eventually face she chose to leave Watson. And after seeing her best friend get killed by Maelstrom she now both hates and fears them. sections.Backstory: Mochi came from a family in japan that would only accept someone being a doctor cause that is what their family was. mochi got her first tanto. mochi wanted to own a restaurant and was taught by her mom how to cook but when her dad learned of this he told her that if she didnt go to school to become a doctor she would be disowned. with no help from family she set out to become a medtech and took a loan to pay for school. tho even after becoming a medtech she still received no help from family with her loan money and after being forced into becoming a doll her father disowned her anyway as that was no what their family was about. she then was sold off to an owner who thought she would be to much work and sold her to a corpo guy named jerry. jerry took her tanto and sold it to make sure he was safe from her. mochi gave into the beatings and abuse for years as she waited for the opportunity to escape. one day she took the chance when she saw it. she ran into night city to hide away from him. after walking around for days she ended up in heywood where she met some new friends. she talked with people and got to know a bleeding heart named diesel who offered to buy her off her owner. after buying her, diesel told her to do whatever she wants. she was confused and then he asked her what she wants to do. she said her dream is to own a japanese style restaurant but is currently made and set up for being a medtech. he told her she can do both and encouraged her to do what makes her happy. she in now opening up a Japanese style restaurant called "Sakura Moon Café" in Heywood. she wishes to just be known as a good cook and her shop to be known while being able to help people. she just wants to help feed and heal people. eventually get her tanto back but is okay with potentially never finding it.

sections.Name: Anne "Paws" (PC character, Butcher boys)
sections.Psychological Profile:
Raised as a soldier her emotions are hard to read.
She can adjust her behavior to her benefit. Act cute when she needs something, act seductive to attract a target, heartless when the situation ask for it.
Having death around her from a young age to her its just part of life. No tears will be spend.
Knowing death can come for her and the people around her at any moment she has a hard time to make true connections.
Her believes makes her selfish. If she has to chose between helping a begger or a drink in the bar, she will pick the drink. If it doesnt benefit her its unlikely she will do anything.

sections.Backstory: Quinn was a middle tier level customer service employee at Nippon Daily living a fairly good life. Go to work, deal with customers that should just zero themselves, coworkers who should not be able to talk, lastly a boss that is always trying to find scapegoats for their problems. A good life in Night City. Until one day during a normal day of work, Quinn was working a high tier level account with the chance of being prompted if everything went well. Their prized Optical cyberware was hacked thus most if not all of the info of the account got leaked and since it was the cyberware was owned by Quinn, he was given the blame for the leaked info and the loss of the account.  From losing the account, Quinn was fired. With that Quinn was sent in a spiral downfall of both finance and mental well being. Quinn lost faith in trusting people who say they have his back and in optical cyberware having them get removed and replaced with medical cyberware but the hacker left a fun surprise. During the surgery to replace the cyberware bug was left corrupting the original chrome and causing damages to the implant area making sure no new cyberware can be installed. Now living on the street he once mock, working to find the hacker that got him fired or regain his former life.
sections.RP Hooks: recovering from surgery and been out of a job for about a month now

sections.Backstory:
**  
D3m0n grew up a slum kid, grew up in a city that didn't matter, scraped by like any other, parents were mostly absent so she had to grow up fast.

Around 20, she met Rafe, ran with a low-tier gang who thought he was hot shit. to her he felt like safety, protection, and belonging. until toxicity slowly crept in and made it like walking on eggshells in hell

she ended up getting wrapped up in his gigs, either got hurt or scarred for life.

Rafe had a habit of stealing D3m0n's money, used it to buy her "gifts" one of them being a single monowire

during a gig she met Julian, got left behind by Rafe and Julian kept her alive, they got close and he showed her the ropes on how to netrun, got her chrome, helped keep her alive.

but it didn't last long, eventually Rafe caught on and began isolating her further, from telling her that Julian's Corpo trash that shouldn't be trusted, to checking messages, to smashing things. While D3m0n thinks they drifted, Julian was ordered back to base.

she kept netrunning anyway, things got worse, one day, she had enough, she needed to disappear, what better place to disappear than Night City? secretly got a lease on an apartment and once it was approved, she gathered whatever she needed and left in the middle of the night, took the first train to Night City.

sections.Psychological Profile: Sandy is a shattered woman who has lost confidence and direction, she is supremely insecure in nearly all aspects besides her fairly robust education and unique skillset.

sections.Total Cyberware Points: N/A
sections.Skills / Talents / Abilities:
Sandy has no cyberware, no firepower, and about as much muscle as a thin sheet of paper. 

What she does have is an advanced education far surpassing the average citizen, little reliance on technology, and funds from various historical societies and other museums who she works for.
sections.Equipment / Armor / Weapons: None
sections.Backstory:
Sandy was once a proud, boisterous and hypocritical member of a gang in Detroit. An anarchist movement governed by popular consensus. 

Born an orphan and adopted into a rich family, Sandy was always a firebrand and a loud mouth. Calling out hypocrisy, shitty attitudes, and unnecessary violence. 

But eventually, her loud mouth and insecurity drove her to pick a fight she couldn't win. 

After being punched by one of her fellow anarchs, she attacked back with vicious ferocity she was nearly beaten to death in a coma that left her paralyzed for several months.

A wealthy middle class family kept her alive out of distant affection, and when she woke up she made a vow to leave it all behind.

She zeroed out her accounts, had her cyberware ripped out and replaced and took over the properties in the night city she had inherited from her family.
sections.RP Hooks: Sandy is designed to be relatively self contained, since I'm not looking to be drawn too much in local plots due to my limited hardware.
